references.text: Applequist, WL. 2015. A brief review of recent controversies in the taxonomy and nomenclature of Sambucus nigra sensu lato. Acta Hortic. 1061: 25-33

Donoghue, MJ, T Eriksson, PA Reeves & RG Olmstead. 2001. Phylogeny and phylogenetic taxonomy of Dipsacales, with special reference to Sinadoxa and Tetradoxa (Adoxaceae). Harvard Papers in Botany 6: 459-480
Kern, JH & CGGJ van Steenis. 1951. Caprifoliaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 4: 175-194

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 10 November 2024

species.notes: Cultivated. In gardens throughout Malesia, naturalized.

species.citation: Reinw. ex Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 657 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 576; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 576; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 191.
species.distribution: India to China, Japan, Java, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Taiwan. BILIRAN, CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Apayao (photos), Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Isabela (photos), Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), South Cotabato, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY.
species.notes: In most of all provinces in Luzon. Everwet primary and secondary forests, thickets, clearings. Fl./fr. Jan-Dec. 100-2000m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 November 2024

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 4c (1909) 329 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 577; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 185. = Viburnum cornutidens Merr. , PJS 26 (1925) 491 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 251; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 185; Type: BS 40309 Ramos & Edaño (US, iso), Luzon: Benguet, Mt Baudan. = Viburnum platyphyllum Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 284 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 577, as ‘pachyphyllum’; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 185.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Benguet, Camarines, Ifugao, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Misamis Occidental, NEGROS.
species.notes: In primary forests. 100-450m, according to Merrill (1923) 1000-1400m.

species.citation: Rolfe , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 21 (1884) 310 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 577; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 190. = Viburnum laxum Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2576. var. luzonicum. Distribution: China, Indochina, Moluccas, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines, Isabela, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, NEGROS. Notes: In thickets and forests. Fl./fr. Jan-Dec. 800-2200m. Native. var. apoense Elmer, LPB 7 (1915) 2577; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 577; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 190. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. ?LUZON: ?Pampanga, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Lanao. Notes: In thickets and forests at medium elevation. Native. var. floribundum (Merr.) J.Kern, Reinwardtia 1 (1951) 163; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 190; ≡ Viburnum floribundum Merr., PJS 4c (1909) 328; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 577. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Abra, Mountain Province, Pampanga. Native. var. sinuatum (Merr.) J.Kern, Reinwardtia 1 (1951) 163; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 190; ≡ Viburnum sinuatum Merr., Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 65; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 578. Distribution: LUZON: Bataan, Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Mountain Province, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, NEGROS. Native.
species.distribution: China, Indochina, Moluccas, Philippines, Taiwan. BILIRAN, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Apayao (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY (photos).

species.citation: Ker Gawl. , Bot. Reg. 6 (1820) t.456 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 577; Kern & Steen. Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 189. = Viburnum zambalense Elmer , LPB 9 (1934) 3181.
species.distribution: Philippines, Sulawesi. BILIRAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Apayao, Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Camarines, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Misamis Oriental (photos), South Cotabato (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY.
species.notes: Viburnum cornutidens and Viburnum platyphyllum have been synonymized with Viburnum odoratissimum following LaFrankie pers. comm. (12-Dec-13). In primary forests, ravines, thickets etc. Fl./fr. Jan-Dec. 1000-2000m.

species.citation: Hemsl. , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 23 (1888) 355 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 578; Kern & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 189. = Viburnum valerianicum Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2578.
species.distribution: China, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Nueva Vizcaya, Pampanga, Rizal.
